nameMeanings.name: Appleton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places in all parts of England named Appleton, from Old English æppeltūn ‘orchard’ (literally ‘apple enclosure’). Apples were particularly important in the Middle Ages as a food for survival through the winter.
History: This surname was brought to North America in 1635 by Samuel Appleton, who migrated from Ipswich, England, to Ipswich, MA.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Moira
meanings: Anglicized form of Irish Máire , now used throughout the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: Mary Appleton was born about 1490, in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with William Theodore Rose 1st of Clava. She died on 27 August 1526, in Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 37.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Appletonは約1490年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストラトフォード オン アボンで生まれた。彼女はWilliam Theodore Rose 1st of Clavaとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1526年8月27日にイギリスイギリススフォルクリットル ワルディングフィールドで37歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Appleton(은)는 대략 1490년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에서 태어났다. 그녀는 William Theodore Rose 1st of Clava(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1526년 8월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 리틀 월딩필드에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.
